metadata.title: Why AI Makes You Better At Exactly the Tasks It Makes You Dangerously Worse At

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The jagged frontier terrifies me because the output looks identical on both sides, and I want a way to locate the boundary before I trust the work. Take two of my real tasks — synthesizing existing industry data versus reasoning about a novel edge case. Help me: (1) explain why the model reliably helps inside the frontier and confidently fails just outside it; (2) show why the output surface gives me zero signal about which zone I'm in; (3) give me an external check I can run — not based on how the answer reads — to decide which side of the frontier a given task sits on.

props.command: The jagged frontier terrifies me because the output looks identical on both sides, and I want a way to locate the boundary before I trust the work. Take two of my real tasks — synthesizing existing industry data versus reasoning about a novel edge case. Help me: (1) explain why the model reliably helps inside the frontier and confidently fails just outside it; (2) show why the output surface gives me zero signal about which zone I'm in; (3) give me an external check I can run — not based on how the answer reads — to decide which side of the frontier a given task sits on.
props.segment: Why AI Makes You Better At

beats.narration_text: In a controlled study, experienced consultants using AI got better results on certain tasks and significantly worse results on others.
beats.scene_description: two consultants at adjacent desks, each reviewing documents; editorial newsprint desaturated treatment; slow push-in toward the papers on their desks
beats.image_prompt: B01, two professional consultants at adjacent desks reviewing documents, professional office setting, editorial desaturated newsprint treatment, flat print lighting, no text legible

beats.narration_text: The troubling part: the quality of the output gave them no signal about which category they were in. Both looked identical on the page.

production_viz.label: two deliverables, one right one wrong, identical surface
production_viz.mechanic: two document chips side by side — both look identical in style and confidence — one teal-backed 'CORRECT', one crimson-backed 'WRONG'; then both are covered with a slate overlay showing they look the same before checking

beats.narration_text: AI should make experienced professionals better at their work. In one category of tasks, it made them worse than working alone. Why didn't the quality of the output tell them?

card.copy: Why didn't the quality of the output tell them which task was failing?

beats.narration_text: The naive expectation: a tool that makes you better at some tasks should signal when it cannot help. The output should feel different when it is wrong.

production_viz.label: expected: wrong output feels different
production_viz.mechanic: two output chips — one teal 'CORRECT', one that should feel crimson 'UNCERTAIN' but instead also appears teal in the naive model; a serif label 'what we expect' points to both

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] the naive expectation: wrong feels different

beats.narration_text: The AI capability frontier is not a smooth line. It is jagged — it runs through every domain, cutting tasks into two zones: inside, where the model reliably helps; outside, where it confidently fails.

production_viz.label: the jagged frontier — two zones
production_viz.mechanic: a horizontal field; a jagged irregular line cuts across it; left of the line labeled 'INSIDE' in teal; right labeled 'OUTSIDE' in crimson; the jaggedness is the point

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] the jagged frontier divides reliable from failing

beats.narration_text: Inside the frontier, the model has seen enough similar cases to reason well. It draws on patterns that generalize. The output is genuinely better than what you would produce alone.

production_viz.label: inside the frontier: reliable assistance
production_viz.mechanic: a teal zone with an 'INSIDE' chip; a task chip enters and exits as an 'IMPROVED OUTPUT' chip; arrows show the quality gain

beats.narration_text: Outside the frontier, the model encounters patterns it cannot reliably apply. But it does not stop. It generates the same fluent, confident output. The surface is identical. The reasoning is wrong.

production_viz.label: outside the frontier: confident failure
production_viz.mechanic: a crimson zone with an 'OUTSIDE' chip; a task chip enters and exits as what looks like an 'OUTPUT' chip — but a crimson 'WRONG' label appears under it; same styling as B06 but with the crimson failure marker

beats.narration_text: The boundary between these two zones is not labeled in the output. It is not labeled anywhere.

card.copy: The boundary is not labeled in the output.

beats.narration_text: Two consultants tackle adjacent problems. One synthesizes existing industry data — inside the frontier. AI output is excellent. The other reasons about a novel domain edge case — outside it. AI output is fluent, confident, and wrong. Both deliverables land on the client's desk looking identical.

production_viz.label: two consultants, two zones, one surface
production_viz.mechanic: two parallel lanes; top lane teal 'INSIDE FRONTIER' — task enters, good output exits; bottom lane crimson 'OUTSIDE FRONTIER' — task enters, wrong output exits styled identically; both outputs shown side by side at the right with identical chips

beats.narration_text: The consultant working inside the frontier gets better. The consultant working outside the frontier gets worse — because she trusts an output that sounds right but is not. The tool improved both their work equally in appearance.

production_viz.label: one gets better, one gets worse — output looks the same
production_viz.mechanic: two consultant chips; one labeled 'BETTER' in teal with an upward arrow; one labeled 'WORSE' in crimson with a downward arrow; both with identical 'POLISHED OUTPUT' chips beside them

beats.narration_text: The implication bites hardest when the outside-frontier task is one you know well. Familiarity breeds trust. You see the output and it rings true — because you are not expert enough in this edge to know what wrong looks like.
beats.scene_description: a consultant leaning over a polished deliverable document, nodding approval, the confidence of approval written on their face; editorial newsprint desaturated treatment
beats.image_prompt: B11, consultant reviewing a polished professional document with approval, pointing at the page, confident expression, editorial desaturated newsprint treatment, flat print lighting, no text legible

beats.narration_text: The move is to know which zone you are in before you trust the output. Inside the frontier: the model has seen this territory. Outside it: it is confabulating fluently. The question to ask is not 'does this look right?' but 'has this specific reasoning been well-tested?'

production_viz.label: the question to ask: which zone am I in?
production_viz.mechanic: the jagged frontier again; a question chip 'which zone?' appears; a teal chip labeled 'WELL-TESTED TERRITORY' on the inside; a crimson chip 'NOVEL EDGE CASE' on the outside; two verification gates labeled differently

beats.narration_text: For well-trodden tasks — synthesis, summarization, structured analysis of familiar data — trust the output more and verify a sample. For novel edge cases, domain boundaries, or tasks you cannot independently judge — treat the output as a draft, not a verdict.

production_viz.label: two verification gates for two zones
production_viz.mechanic: two rows: top row teal 'INSIDE' with gate labeled 'SAMPLE CHECK'; bottom row crimson 'OUTSIDE' with gate labeled 'TREAT AS DRAFT'; chips flow through each gate appropriately

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] two verification strategies for two zones

beats.narration_text: Worked example: two analysts get the same assignment — summarize twelve industry reports. One uses AI on the synthesis, samples three reports, confirms the findings. One uses AI on a novel edge-case forecast nobody has modeled before. Only the second one is outside the frontier. Both outputs look the same.

production_viz.label: same assignment, different frontier zones
production_viz.mechanic: two task chips side by side: 'SYNTHESIS — inside frontier' in teal and 'NOVEL FORECAST — outside frontier' in crimson; both produce identically-styled output chips; the teal one has a 'VERIFIED' tick; the crimson has a hidden 'WRONG' marker

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] worked example — same surface, different zones

beats.narration_text: The practice: before you act on an AI output, ask one question. Is this the kind of task the AI has seen a thousand times? If yes — verify a sample. If no — treat it as a starting draft and apply your own domain judgment before it goes anywhere.

card.copy: Has the AI seen this a thousand times? If not, treat it as a draft.

beats.narration_text: AI did not make them worse by being broken. It made them worse by being trusted in the wrong zone. The capability frontier is real. The output never shows you where you are standing on it.

card.copy: The frontier is real. The output won't show you where you stand.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. The practice: before you act on an AI output, ask one question. Is this the kind of task the AI has seen a thousand times? If yes — verify a sample. If no — treat it as a starting draft and apply your own domain judgment before it goes anywhere. AI did not make them worse by being broken. It made them worse by being trusted in the wrong zone. The capability frontier is real. The output never shows you where you are standing on it.

props.artifactHeading: Why AI Makes You Better At Exactly the
artifactLines: Worked example: two analysts get the same assignment — summarize twelve industry reports.
The practice: before you act on an AI output, ask one question.
AI did not make them worse by being broken.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Why AI Makes You Better At Exactly the Tasks It Makes You Dangerously ] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
props.segment: Why AI Makes You Better At
